dog_skin_diseases.name: Demodicosis
dog_skin_diseases.description: A skin condition caused by the overgrowth of Demodex mites, usually affecting puppies or immunocompromised dogs.
treatment: Oral or topical anti-parasitic medications (e.g., ivermectin, moxidectin)
dog_skin_diseases.when_to_see_vet: Seek veterinary care if you see progressive hair loss, severe inflammation, or if the dog appears uncomfortable.

dog_skin_diseases.name: Dermatitis
dog_skin_diseases.description: A general term for skin inflammation, often caused by allergies, environmental irritants, or infections.
dog_skin_diseases.when_to_see_vet: Consult a veterinarian if symptoms persist for more than a few days, worsen, or cause severe itching.

dog_skin_diseases.name: Fungal_infections
dog_skin_diseases.description: Skin infections caused by fungi, commonly yeast (Malassezia) or dermatophytes.
dog_skin_diseases.when_to_see_vet: Vet attention is needed if the dog shows persistent itchiness, odor, or the infection spreads.

dog_skin_diseases.name: Healthy
dog_skin_diseases.description: Indicates the dog’s skin appears normal with no signs of disease.
dog_skin_diseases.when_to_see_vet: No veterinary attention needed unless symptoms change or abnormalities appear.

dog_skin_diseases.name: Hypersensitivity
dog_skin_diseases.description: An exaggerated immune response to allergens such as flea bites, food, or environmental factors.
dog_skin_diseases.when_to_see_vet: Vet visit is necessary if itching is severe or stops the dog from resting, eating, or behaving normally.

dog_skin_diseases.name: Ringworm
dog_skin_diseases.description: A contagious fungal infection that affects the skin, hair, and nails of dogs and humans.
causes: Exposure to infected animals or objects
dog_skin_diseases.when_to_see_vet: See a vet immediately because ringworm is highly contagious to pets and humans.
